title: Choice of Self Mastery
content: Everyone is talking about the systems that hold us back: the wage gap, the lack of freedom, the rigged game. And they’re right. But there’s a trap here: we spend so much time fighting for the 'win' that we’ve forgotten how to advance ourselves. We’re told that every 'no' is an opportunity. But if you don’t understand who you are or what you actually want, that opportunity is just noise. It’s like an athlete training just to stay in second place, or a cook who never intends to run their own kitchen. Why bother? The truth is that self-mastery and professional growth are the same thing. You can’t have one without the other. If we finally beat the system but we aren't grounded in who we are, we’ll squander the victory the second we get it. We’ll have the equality we fought for and no idea what to do with it. Choosing to do one thing well, to really master a craft, is what builds your identity. It’s how you learn to be a 'good person' in practice, not just in theory. Success isn't just about changing the world. It’s about making sure you're someone worth being when you finally get to where you're going.

title: The War Within
content: The most difficult rule to follow is often the first one: you must respect yourself...
